contradictions.evidence_span_b: Exception requests are submitted to the Cloud Governance Board through the Infrastructure Exception form, with a named business owner and compensating control.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires exceptions to be submitted to the Information Security team, while Claim B requires exceptions to be submitted to the Cloud Governance Board.

contradictions.evidence_span_a: All storage volumes, object storage buckets, and managed databases must enable encryption at rest using AES-256 with customer-managed keys for any resource holding Conﬁdential or Restricted data.

contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires customer-managed keys for encryption at rest, while Claim B does not specify key management.

contradictions.claim_a: REQUIREMENTS Password Standards: Minimum 14 characters for standard user accounts and 18 characters for privileged/administrative accounts. Passwords must be rotated every 90 days for privileged accounts; standard user accounts follow a 180-day rotation cycle where risk-based authentication is not enabled. Multi-Factor Authentication: Mandatory for all remote network access, all privileged accounts, and all access to systems classiﬁed as Conﬁdential or Restricted. Approved methods are hardware security keys (FIDO2) or authenticator app TOTP codes. SMS-based MFA is not permitted as a standalone method for privileged access. Remote Access: All connections to internal resources from unmanaged or public networks must route through the corporate VPN using IPSec with AES-256 encryption. Split tunneling is disabled by default. Encryption: Data at rest classiﬁed as Conﬁdential or higher must use AES-256 encryption. Data in transit must use TLS 1.2 or higher; TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are deprecated and blocked at the gateway. Session Management: Privileged administrative sessions time out after 15 minutes of inactivity. Standard user sessions time out after 30 minutes. Logging and Monitoring: Security-relevant logs (authentication, privileged access, conﬁguration changes) are retained for a minimum of 13 months and forwarded to the centralized SIEM within 5 minutes of generation.
contradictions.claim_b: provisioning. Shadow IT cloud accounts opened outside the corporate billing hierarchy are prohibited. REQUIREMENTS Account Provisioning: All cloud accounts are provisioned through the centralized Landing Zone process and linked to the corporate identity provider via SAML federation. Standalone root or account-owner credentials are prohibited for daily use; root credentials are sealed in the enterprise secrets vault and accessed only under break-glass procedures requiring dual authorization. Authentication: All console and CLI access requires multi-factor authentication using a hardware security key or authenticator app. Programmatic access uses short-lived credentials issued via the identity provider's federation role, with a maximum session token life of 4 hours. Long-lived static access keys are disallowed except for documented legacy integrations with an approved sunset date. Access Reviews: Privileged cloud roles (Administrator, Owner, and equivalent) are reviewed every 60 days by the Cloud Operations team. Standard developer access follows a 6-month review cycle aligned to project assignment changes. Encryption: All storage volumes, object storage buckets, and managed databases must enable encryption at rest using AES-256 with customer-managed keys for any resource holding Conﬁdential or Restricted data. Data in transit between services must use TLS 1.2 or higher. Keys are rotated every 12 months or immediately upon suspected compromise.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Passwords must be rotated every 90 days for privileged accounts; standard user accounts follow a 180-day rotation cycle where risk-based authentication is not enabled.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: All cloud accounts are provisioned through the centralized Landing Zone process and linked to the corporate identity provider via SAML federation.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires privileged accounts to rotate passwords every 90 days, while Claim B does not specify a rotation period for cloud accounts, potentially leading to inconsistent password management.

contradictions.claim_a: December 31 each year. Non-completion results in automatic account suspension pending remediation. Violations of this policy may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination, and may be referred to Legal and HR for further review. Third-party vendors found in violation are subject to contract review by Vendor Management. EXCEPTIONS Exceptions to any requirement in this policy must be submitted through the Security Exception Request Form to the Information Security team, with a documented business justiﬁcation and proposed compensating controls. Exceptions require CISO or delegate approval, are valid for a maximum of 12 months, and must be reassessed every 6 months for continued necessity. REVIEW PROCESS This policy is reviewed annually by the Information Security Governance Committee. Material changes to control requirements require joint sign-off from the CISO and General Counsel. Minor clariﬁcations or formatting updates may be approved directly by the Director of Information Security. Conﬁdential — Internal Use Only. Property of Meridian Dynamics Corporation. Unauthorized reproduction or distribution outside the organization is prohibited.
contradictions.claim_b: within their assigned business units. People Managers are responsible for timely notiﬁcation of employment changes and for completing performance documentation on schedule. Employees are responsible for reviewing and acknowledging policy updates within 10 business days of notiﬁcation. COMPLIANCE Managers who fail to submit termination notiﬁcations within required timeframes are subject to review by the HR Business Partner and, for repeated instances, escalation to their department VP. Falsiﬁcation of personnel records or background check information is grounds for immediate termination. This policy is audited annually by Internal Audit as part of the broader People Operations control review. EXCEPTIONS Requests for exceptions to standard PTO accrual, probationary terms, or remote work eligibility must be submitted in writing to the employee's HR Business Partner with department head endorsement. Exceptions affecting more than one employee within the same team require VP of HR approval and are documented in the HR Policy Exception Log, retained for the duration of the affected employee's tenure plus 3 years. REVIEW PROCESS This policy is reviewed annually by the HR Policy Council in coordination with Legal and Compliance. Changes affecting statutory obligations (leave entitlements, termination notice periods) are fast-tracked outside the standard annual cycle upon conﬁrmed changes in applicable law.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Exceptions to any requirement in this policy must be submitted through the Security Exception Request Form to the Information Security team, with a documented business justiﬁcation and proposed compensating controls.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Requests for exceptions to standard PTO accrual, probationary terms, or remote work eligibility must be submitted in writing to the employee's HR Business Partner with department head endorsement.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires exceptions to be submitted through the Security Exception Request Form, while Claim B requires exceptions to be submitted in writing to the HR Business Partner. This discrepancy may lead to inconsistent exception handling processes.

contradictions.claim_a: provisioning. Shadow IT cloud accounts opened outside the corporate billing hierarchy are prohibited. REQUIREMENTS Account Provisioning: All cloud accounts are provisioned through the centralized Landing Zone process and linked to the corporate identity provider via SAML federation. Standalone root or account-owner credentials are prohibited for daily use; root credentials are sealed in the enterprise secrets vault and accessed only under break-glass procedures requiring dual authorization. Authentication: All console and CLI access requires multi-factor authentication using a hardware security key or authenticator app. Programmatic access uses short-lived credentials issued via the identity provider's federation role, with a maximum session token life of 4 hours. Long-lived static access keys are disallowed except for documented legacy integrations with an approved sunset date. Access Reviews: Privileged cloud roles (Administrator, Owner, and equivalent) are reviewed every 60 days by the Cloud Operations team. Standard developer access follows a 6-month review cycle aligned to project assignment changes. Encryption: All storage volumes, object storage buckets, and managed databases must enable encryption at rest using AES-256 with customer-managed keys for any resource holding Conﬁdential or Restricted data. Data in transit between services must use TLS 1.2 or higher. Keys are rotated every 12 months or immediately upon suspected compromise.
contradictions.claim_b: INFORMATION SECURITY POLICY Document Version: 3.2 Effective Date: March 1, 2026 Review Date: March 1, 2027 Classiﬁcation: Internal Use Only Policy Owner: Chief Information Security Ofﬁcer, Meridian Dynamics Corporation PURPOSE This policy establishes the minimum requirements for protecting the conﬁdentiality, integrity, and availability of Meridian Dynamics Corporation's information assets, including data, applications, endpoints, network infrastructure, and cloud-hosted environments. It exists to reduce exposure to unauthorized access, data loss, and service disruption across all business units. SCOPE This policy applies to all full-time employees, contingent workers, contractors, and third-party service providers who access Meridian Dynamics systems, networks, or data, regardless of location or device ownership. It covers company-managed and BYOD endpoints, all production and non- production environments, and any SaaS platform where corporate data is stored or processed. POLICY STATEMENTS Access to company systems is granted on a least-privilege basis and tied to a documented business justiﬁcation. All access is logged, attributable to an individual account, and subject to periodic review. Shared or generic credentials are prohibited except for break-glass emergency accounts, which require CISO pre-approval and are rotated immediately after use. REQUIREMENTS Password Standards: Minimum 14 characters for standard user accounts and 18 characters for
contradictions.evidence_span_a: REQUIREMENTS Account Provisioning: All cloud accounts are provisioned through the centralized Landing Zone process and linked to the corporate identity provider via SAML federation.
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Password Standards: Minimum 14 characters for standard user accounts and 18 characters for
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires all cloud accounts to be provisioned through the centralized Landing Zone process, while Claim B does not mention this requirement, instead focusing on password standards.

contradictions.claim_b: CLOUD INFRASTRUCTURE POLICY Document Version: 1.8 Effective Date: April 15, 2026 Review Date: April 15, 2027 Classiﬁcation: Internal Use Only Policy Owner: Head of Cloud Platform Engineering, Meridian Dynamics Corporation PURPOSE This policy establishes the standards for provisioning, securing, and operating cloud infrastructure across Meridian Dynamics Corporation's production and non-production environments. It governs how workloads, data, and identities are managed within approved cloud platforms and sets the operational baseline for cost control, resilience, and security posture. SCOPE This policy applies to all cloud accounts, subscriptions, and resources provisioned under Meridian Dynamics billing structures, including infrastructure-as-a-service, platform-as-a-service, and serverless workloads. It covers Engineering, Data Platform, and DevOps teams, and extends to any business unit provisioning cloud resources for departmental use, regardless of funding source. POLICY STATEMENTS Amazon Web Services is the company's primary approved cloud provider for production workloads. Microsoft Azure is approved as a secondary provider for workloads with existing Microsoft licensing dependencies or regional data residency requirements. Use of Google Cloud Platform or any other provider requires written justiﬁcation and Cloud Governance Board approval prior to provisioning. Shadow IT cloud accounts opened outside the corporate billing hierarchy are prohibited.
contradictions.evidence_span_a: Password Standards: Minimum 14 characters for standard user accounts and 18 characters for
contradictions.evidence_span_b: Use of Google Cloud Platform or any other provider requires written justiﬁcation and Cloud Governance Board approval prior to provisioning.
contradictions.rationale: Claim A requires a least-privilege basis for access to company systems, while Claim B allows for the use of Google Cloud Platform with written justification and approval, which may not adhere to the least-privilege principle.
